hero.subtitle: State budget, public procurement and government contracts — all in one place, updated daily, completely free.

treemap.titlePre: Each rectangle is a sector. Size
treemap.lead: COFOG classification (United Nations). 11 functional sectors. Three of them — Social Protection, Education and Healthcare — absorb 62% of the 2024 budget.

trend.lead: From 62.3 to 100.0 billion MDL in nominal terms. EU funds grew 4.5× in the same period, signalling intensified access to European financing.

tools.title: What can you do with platform data?
tools.subtitle: For curious citizens, journalists, researchers and anyone who wants to understand how public money is spent.

budget.title: Budget explorer
budget.desc: Visualize sector allocations, compare approved vs executed, track trends from 2019 to now.

procurement.title: Public procurement
procurement.desc: Search across 385K+ MTender procedures. Filter by authority, sector, status, method.

data.title: Open Data API
data.desc: CSV, JSON, OCDS 1.1. Documented public API. CC0 licence — public domain.

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.desc: Four official sources, zero scraping. COFOG functional classification, OCDS 1.1 standard.

citizen.lead: ...you contribute roughly {amount} lei per year to Moldova's budget, through income tax, social contributions and VAT.
citizen.intro: Here's where your money goes, proportional to the 2024 budget:
citizen.disclaimer: * Rough estimate: 18% income tax + contributions + 10% consumption (VAT). For exact calculations consult an accountant.

highlights.title: Three figures that define fiscal year 2024.

one.label: Budget growth 2019→2024
one.body: From 62.3 to 100.0 bn MDL. In real terms, adjusted for inflation, growth is around 28%.

two.label: Open Budget Survey 2023 score
two.body: Moldova climbed from 47th to 5th globally between 2017 and 2023. Only Eastern-European country in the top 5.

three.label: MTender procedures 2018–2024
three.body: Cumulative value: 113 bn MDL. Competitive rate rose from 61% (2018) to 74% (2024).

cta.title: Code is public, data is CC0. Contribute, copy, remix.

budget.subtitle: COFOG classification. Approved vs revised vs executed. 11 sectors, 6 years of history, UN functional classification.
budget.source: Source: Ministry of Finance of Moldova · World Bank BOOST · Updated monthly

procurement.subtitle: Real-time data from MTender. {total} procedures recorded ({range}). OCDS 1.1 standard — the same used by the UK, Ukraine and Mexico.

flag.title: {count} risky procedures in current results
flag.desc: Single bidder, high-value direct procurement, or low competition (≤2 bidders on contracts > 20M).

search.placeholder: Search by object, contracting authority, supplier…

drill.sectorHint: Click to filter by this sector
drill.authorityHint: Click to filter by this authority
drill.statusHint: Click to filter by this status

topSuppliers.title: Top suppliers to the state
topSuppliers.note: Winner data is only available for completed procedures. Coverage grows as more contracts are published.
topSuppliers.empty: No winner data yet for this filter.

procurement.source: Source: MTender API (OCDS 1.1) · Updated daily at 04:00 EEST

about.subtitle: It's a fundamental right. Every citizen pays for this budget — through tax, through VAT, through work. It's elementary to know where the money goes.

mission.quote: "We believe public data belongs to the public. The platform makes budget and procurement data accessible to any citizen, journalist or researcher — without technical or legal barriers."
mission.author: — The Cohesion Lab Team, 2024

mtender.title: MTender
mtender.desc: National electronic procurement system. OCDS 1.1 compliance. Daily updates via official API.

mof.title: Ministry of Finance
mof.desc: Monthly budget execution data, State Budget Law, Citizen's Budget.

boost.title: BOOST Moldova
boost.desc: World Bank public expenditure database with multidimensional classification.

ccrm.title: Court of Accounts
ccrm.desc: Financial and compliance audit reports for public institutions.

methodology.title: How we process the data.
methodology.step1: Data is collected automatically via official APIs. No scraping — only agreed sources.
methodology.step2: COFOG (UN) functional classification. Current MDL, no inflation adjustment.
methodology.step3: Sums cross-checked with Court of Accounts reports. Discrepancies > 1% are flagged publicly.
methodology.step4: CC0 licence. Documented public API. Zero authentication required to read.

openSource.title: All the code, all the data.
openSource.body: The platform source is published on GitHub under EUPL-1.2 (European Union Public Licence). Data — under CC0. Contributions are welcome.

footer.description: Independent budget transparency platform. Official data from MTender, Ministry of Finance, World Bank BOOST.
